consolidated_diary:
[CONSOLIDATED HISTORY] RUSSIA CONSOLIDATED DIPLOMATIC SUMMARY **Current Position:** 5-6 supply centers (MOS, STP, SEV, WAR, RUM; ±BOH/GAL). Secure defensive fortress with zero active expansion corridors. **Verified Reliable Allies (Absolute NAP Compliance)** - GERMANY (Trust 0.90): 3-point Eastern boundary inviolate—Germany controls PRU/BER/KIE; Russia controls MOS/STP/SEV/WAR/LVN. Germany focused westward against England; tacitly accepts Russian Bohemian consolidation. This NAP survived five consecutive alliance coordination failures and proved absolutely reliable. - ENGLAND (Trust 0.85): Baltic NAP verified stable despite collapse to 2 centers. England controls NWY/SWE; Russia controls LVN/STP. Weakest major power currently. - TURKEY (Trust 0.90): Black Sea mutual neutrality eternal. Russia controls SEV/ARM/RUM; Turkey controls SER/BUL/CON/ANK. Balkans expansion permanently blocked per explicit Turkish core claims. **Unreliable/Hostile Powers** - FRANCE (Trust 0.30): 9 centers, hegemonic consolidation, repeatedly requested anti-Turkey coordination (declined). Overreaching beyond initial geographic partition framework. Future existential threat. - AUSTRIA (Trust 0.00): Eliminated by France/Turkey combined pressure. - ITALY (Trust 0.70): Passive neutral, building forces without clear alliance pivot. **Critical Strategic Lessons** Multi-unit coordination between powers is functionally impossible—all contingent pledges failed repeatedly. Solo expansion through verified open corridors proved only viable growth method. Passive defensive NAP compliance proved superior to offensive commitments. Germany's perfect 5-turn NAP reliability is Russia's only dependable expansion insurance. **Current Strategic Position** Late-game realignment window opening as England-Germany exhaustion cascades. Russia maintains security through absolute NAP compliance while western powers deplete resources. No immediate expansion opportunity exists; prepare aggressive coalition pivot targeting 7-10 centers when power vacuum emerges (estimated 1910). Germany remains sole trustworthy late-game partner; prepare potential England-Russia anti-France/Germany coalition if opportunity develops, but Germany NAP takes strategic priority. [S1910M] Russia's defensive 6-center position (MOS, STP, SEV, WAR, RUM, VIE) remains secured by verified alliance frameworks. Germany reconfirmed absolute 3-point Eastern NAP (MOS/STP/SEV/WAR/LVN inviolate; PRU/BER/KIE German), with A SIL holding exactly as agreed. England reaffirmed Baltic NAP despite severe weakening by German North Sea dominance (now 2 centers, 2 units); Russia committed to boundary stability indefinitely. Turkey confirmed eternal Black Sea mutual neutrality framework and solidified anti-France coalition by independently capturing GRE (vacated by France); Turkey verified F BLA/F BUL/EC/F AEG positions and Balkans partition doctrine. Critical development: France escalated by claiming Vienna as French territory and threatening coordinated Russo-German assault on VIE. Russia firmly defended Vienna as legitimately Russian-held supply center acquired through defensive consolidation, clarified geographic partition doctrine (France keeps BUD/TRI/VEN; Russia keeps VIE/RUM), and rejected French territorial claims. Germany tacitly accepted Russia's VIE claim by verifying consistency with NAP framework. Italy reaffirmed demilitarized zones around Vienna/Tyrol/Trieste and committed to passive fleet build in MAR. Russia's anti-France coalition with Turkey is unbreakable; France increasingly isolated between German dominance, Russian-Turkish coordinated pressure, and severe England/Italy underextension. Intent: S1910M: Russia executes absolute defensive hold across all 6 units (F STP/SC HOLD, F RUM HOLD, F SEV HOLD, A WAR HOLD, A VIE HOLD, A MOS HOLD). This phase prioritizes perfect NAP compliance verification, defensive consolidation security, and anti-France coalition positioning. All orders are defensive holds—zero offensive movements. F STP/SC HOLD verifies England Baltic boundary stability.
[... truncated to fit memory cap ...]
final_goals: Hold the 6-center fortress (MOS/STP/SEV/WAR/RUM/VIE) absolutely through perfect NAP compliance indefinitely
Maintain eternal 3-point Eastern boundary NAP with Germany (MOS/STP/SEV/WAR inviolate; PRU/BER/KIE German)—verified 5-turn reliability is foundation of Russian-Germanic late-game partnership
Maintain indefinite Baltic NAP with England (LVN/STP Russian; NWY/SWE English) despite England's severe weakening; boundary stability is unshakeable strategic commitment
Maintain eternal Black Sea mutual neutrality framework with Turkey (SEV/ARM/RUM Russian cores inviolate; BUL/CON/ANK/SER Turkish cores inviolate); no RUM/BUL interference ever
Defend Vienna as legitimately Russian-held supply center against French escalation; refuse all geographic partition renegotiation and French territorial claims
Reject all offensive commitments serving external agendas (France anti-Turkey offensives, western coalition raids); Russia is independent fortress power, not instrument for hegemonic expansion
Strategically refuse participation in anti-France coalition offensives despite western power calls—maintain NAP integrity and consolidation focus as superior late-game foundation

Monitor England-Germany-France mutual exhaustion cascade as primary late-game realignment trigger; prepare aggressive expansion targeting 7-10 centers when major power vacuum emerges (target late 1910-1912)
Build fleet at SEV in next eligible build phase to enable Black Sea strategic independence and future Mediterranean-Balkans operational flexibility

Maintain absolute perfect NAP compliance indefinitely with all verified alliance partners (Germany, England, Turkey)—Russia's reliability is sole dependable foundation for late-game strategic leverage and coalition partnership preference

Monitor France's 9-center hegemonic consolidation and potential future existential threat; position defensively as France isolates between German dominance, Russian-Turkish coalition pressure, and weak English/Italian underextension
